rag_builder_best_practices.overview: Best practices for building production-ready RAG systems using IBM Watsonx and vector database technologies.

principle.name: Optimal Chunk Size Selection
principle.description: Choose chunk size based on use case and model context window

guidelines.guideline: For code: Use semantic chunking by function/class
Always include overlap (10-20%) to preserve context

principle.example:
from langchain.text_splitter import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter
# Optimal chunking for Q&A splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter( chunk_size=400, # ~300 tokens chunk_overlap=50, # 12.5% overlap separators=["

", "
", ". ", " ", ""], length_function=len )
chunks = splitter.split_documents(documents)

principle.name: Metadata Preservation
principle.description: Maintain rich metadata for filtering and citation
principle.example: # Enrich chunks with metadata for chunk in chunks: chunk.metadata.update({ "source": document.source, "title": document.metadata.get("title"), "page": chunk.metadata.get("page"), "section": extract_section_heading(chunk), "doc_type": document.metadata.get("type"), "ingestion_date": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(), "chunk_id": generate_chunk_id(document, chunk) })

principle.name: Use IBM Watsonx Embeddings
principle.description: Leverage IBM Watsonx embedding models for optimal performance

implementation.practice: Use slate models for English text retrieval
Monitor embedding API usage and costs

principle.example:
from ibm_watsonx_ai import Embeddings from ibm_watsonx_ai.foundation_models.utils.enums import EmbeddingTypes
# Configure IBM Watsonx embeddings embeddings = Embeddings( model_id=EmbeddingTypes.IBM_SLATE_125M_ENG, credentials={ "url": os.getenv("WATSONX_URL"), "apikey": os.getenv("WATSONX_API_KEY") }, project_id=os.getenv("WATSONX_PROJECT_ID") )
# Batch embed for efficiency texts = [chunk.page_content for chunk in chunks] vectors = embeddings.embed_documents(texts, batch_size=100)
# Cache embeddings cache_embeddings(chunks, vectors)

principle.name: Embedding Dimension Consistency
principle.description: Ensure embedding dimensions match vector database schema

validation.check: Test with sample embeddings before bulk ingestion

principle.name: Index Selection and Configuration
principle.description: Choose appropriate index type for use case
principle.milvus_configuration:
# Milvus collection configuration from pymilvus import Collection, CollectionSchema, FieldSchema, DataType
fields = [ FieldSchema(name="id", dtype=DataType.INT64, is_primary=True, auto_id=True), FieldSchema(name="embedding", dtype=DataType.FLOAT_VECTOR, dim=768), FieldSchema(name="text", dtype=DataType.VARCHAR, max_length=65535), FieldSchema(name="metadata", dtype=DataType.JSON) ]
schema = CollectionSchema(fields, description="RAG knowledge base") collection = Collection("knowledge_base", schema)
# Create HNSW index index_params = { "metric_type": "COSINE", "index_type": "HNSW", "params": {"M": 16, "efConstruction": 256} } collection.create_index("embedding", index_params)

principle.name: Hybrid Search Implementation
principle.description: Combine vector search with keyword search for better results

techniques.technique:
# Expand query with LLM from ibm_watsonx_ai.foundation_models import Model
llm = Model( model_id="ibm/granite-13b-chat-v2", credentials=credentials, project_id=project_id )
expansion_prompt = f""" Generate 3 alternative phrasings of this question: Question: {query}
Alternative phrasings: 1. 2. 3. """
expanded_queries = llm.generate_text(expansion_prompt) # Search with all query variations

principle.name: Reranking for Precision
principle.example:
from sentence_transformers import CrossEncoder
# Initialize reranker reranker = CrossEncoder('cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2')
def rerank_results(query: str, results: list, top_k: int = 5): # Create query-document pairs pairs = [[query, doc['text']] for doc in results]
# Score all pairs scores = reranker.predict(pairs)
# Sort by score and return top_k scored_results = list(zip(results, scores)) scored_results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
return [doc for doc, score in scored_results[:top_k]]

principle.name: Metadata Filtering
principle.description: Use metadata to narrow search scope
principle.example:
# Filter by metadata def search_with_filters(query: str, filters: dict, top_k: int = 10): query_vector = embeddings.embed_query(query)
# Build filter expression filter_expr = " AND ".join([ f"{key} == '{value}'" for key, value in filters.items() ])
results = vector_db.search( query_vector, filter=filter_expr, top_k=top_k )
return results
# Example: Search only in specific document type results = search_with_filters( query="What is the refund policy?", filters={"doc_type": "policy", "language": "en"}, top_k=5 )

principle.name: Prompt Engineering for RAG
principle.description: Design effective prompts that leverage retrieved context

principle.name: Context Window Management
principle.description: Optimize context to fit within LLM token limits

strategies.strategy: Compress context by extracting key sentences
Use sliding window for long documents

principle.name: Comprehensive RAG Evaluation
principle.description: Measure both retrieval and generation quality

principle.name: FastMCP Server Implementation
principle.description: Build MCP servers for RAG tools using FastMCP

pitfall.mistake: Chunks too large or too small
pitfall.consequence: Poor retrieval quality or loss of context
pitfall.solution: Test different chunk sizes, aim for 200-500 tokens for Q&A
Cache embeddings, batch process, monitor usage
